title: Bridge Paper: Psychosis and urbanicity: a review of the recent literature from epidemiology to neurourbanism.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Ecology/Animism** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Nature connectedness — biophilia hypothesis, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience.
**Phase context:** nature connectedness, biophilia, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience, nature exposure

Paper: Psychosis and urbanicity: a review of the recent literature from epidemiology to neurourbanism.
DOI: 10.1097/YCO.0000000000000486
Abstract: PURPOSE OF REVIEW: Epidemiological studies associate city living with an elevated psychosis risk. Urban (social/economic) stress and exposure to environmental toxins, pollution or disease agents have been proposed to underlie this association. This review provides an update on the recent evidence (May 2017 - November 2018).
